title: Describe physical infrastructure connections of WLAN components

questions.question: You need more bandwidth to your wireless controller from the router. Currently you have one Gigabit Ethernet connection in use and both your router and wireless controller have another available Gigabit Ethernet connection. What can you do to get more bandwidth?
choices: Nothing. Routers cannot aggregate bandwidth from multiple connections.
Use RIP to balance the bandwidth.
Bundle both Gigabit Ethernet connections in an EtherChannel.
Use the wireless controller to perform inter-VLAN routing.
questions.explanation: A WLC connection can use EtherChannel/LAG to increase bandwidth.

correct.explanation: A WLC connection can use EtherChannel/LAG to increase bandwidth.

incorrect.explanation: Nothing. Routers cannot aggregate bandwidth from multiple connections. misses what this question tests. A WLC connection can use EtherChannel/LAG to increase bandwidth.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Nothing. Routers cannot aggregate bandwi…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Use RIP to balance the bandwidth. misses what this question tests. A WLC connection can use EtherChannel/LAG to increase bandwidth.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Use RIP to balance the bandwidth." without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Use the wireless controller to perform inter-VLAN routing. misses what this question tests. A WLC connection can use EtherChannel/LAG to increase bandwidth.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Use the wireless controller to perform i…" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Match band, range, and standard to the scenario — WEP is never the right modern answer.

questions.question: You are connecting a WLC to the network and anticipate requiring several different network segments for voice and data. Which type of port should you configure on the switch?
questions.explanation: A WLC link should be a trunk when several VLANs must be carried.

correct.explanation: A WLC link should be a trunk when several VLANs must be carried.

incorrect.explanation: Access port misses what this question tests. A WLC link should be a trunk when several VLANs must be carried.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Access port" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Voice port misses what this question tests. A WLC link should be a trunk when several VLANs must be carried.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Voice port" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Routed switch port misses what this question tests. A WLC link should be a trunk when several VLANs must be carried.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Routed switch port" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Mark inside/outside first. PAT = many inside hosts sharing one outside global with unique ports.

questions.question: You are connecting a Cisco WLC to a non-Cisco switch and need to aggregate two ports between the two devices. Which type of link should you research on the non-Cisco device?
questions.explanation: WLCs use LAG, not LACP/PAgP, for bundling links.

correct.explanation: WLCs use LAG, not LACP/PAgP, for bundling links.

incorrect.explanation: LACP misses what this question tests. WLCs use LAG, not LACP/PAgP, for bundling links.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "LACP" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: PAgP misses what this question tests. WLCs use LAG, not LACP/PAgP, for bundling links.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "PAgP" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: PortChannel misses what this question tests. WLCs use LAG, not LACP/PAgP, for bundling links.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "PortChannel" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: EtherChannel is one logical link to STP — LACP (802.3ad) is standards-based; PAgP is Cisco-proprietary.

questions.question: How is traffic load-balanced from a WLC to a switch using LAG?
questions.explanation: WLC LAG load balancing is hash-based.

correct.explanation: WLC LAG load balancing is hash-based.

incorrect.explanation: Round robin misses what this question tests. WLC LAG load balancing is hash-based.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Round robin" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: First in, first out (FIFO) misses what this question tests. WLC LAG load balancing is hash-based.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "First in, first out (FIFO)" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Spill and fill misses what this question tests. WLC LAG load balancing is hash-based.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Spill and fill" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: This stem tests wlc connections — exam distractors swap similar terms; anchor on: WLC LAG load balancing is hash-based.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of ports you can bundle in a LAG between a WLC and a switch?
questions.explanation: A WLC LAG supports up to eight bundled ports.

correct.explanation: A WLC LAG supports up to eight bundled ports.

incorrect.explanation: 16 misses what this question tests. A WLC LAG supports up to eight bundled ports.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "16" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: 4 misses what this question tests. A WLC LAG supports up to eight bundled ports.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "4" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: 2 misses what this question tests. A WLC LAG supports up to eight bundled ports.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "2" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: This stem tests wlc connections — exam distractors swap similar terms; anchor on: A WLC LAG supports up to eight bundled ports.

questions.question: You currently have a WLC with only two physical access ports. One port is used for guest network access and the other port is used for your business communications. You need to add several other networks to the WLC for your manufacturing and quality control departments. What is the simplest and best way to achieve this?
choices: Upgrade the WLC to accommodate more ports.
Convert the current access ports to LAGs.
Convert one of the current access ports to a trunk.
Add a second WLC to accommodate the new departments.
questions.explanation: Converting a WLC access port to a trunk allows multiple VLANs over one physical port.

correct.explanation: Converting a WLC access port to a trunk allows multiple VLANs over one physical port.

incorrect.explanation: Upgrade the WLC to accommodate more ports. misses what this question tests. Converting a WLC access port to a trunk allows multiple VLANs over one physical port.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Upgrade the WLC to accommodate more port…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Convert the current access ports to LAGs. misses what this question tests. Converting a WLC access port to a trunk allows multiple VLANs over one physical port.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Convert the current access ports to LAGs…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Add a second WLC to accommodate the new departments. misses what this question tests. Converting a WLC access port to a trunk allows multiple VLANs over one physical port.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Add a second WLC to accommodate the new …" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Access = one VLAN; trunk tags frames. Native VLAN must match on both ends or you get silent mis-forwarding.

questions.question: You have been asked by your manager to configure a port for a new WAP to be installed for your WLC. How should you configure the port?
questions.explanation: Controller-based AP switch ports are commonly configured as trunks to carry management and data VLANs.

correct.explanation: Controller-based AP switch ports are commonly configured as trunks to carry management and data VLANs.

incorrect.explanation: Access port misses what this question tests. Controller-based AP switch ports are commonly configured as trunks to carry management and data VLANs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Access port" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Wireless port misses what this question tests. Controller-based AP switch ports are commonly configured as trunks to carry management and data VLANs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Wireless port" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: LAG port misses what this question tests. Controller-based AP switch ports are commonly configured as trunks to carry management and data VLANs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "LAG port" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Access = one VLAN; trunk tags frames. Native VLAN must match on both ends or you get silent mis-forwarding.
